source_jobs.title: Finance Manager/Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)
We’re a team of ~50 people who’ve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. We’re a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist http://www.italist.com, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com and Verishop https://www.instagram.com/verishop/?hl=en, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight https://stylight.com and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai https://shopbonsai.ca, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress https://everpress.com and DOZR http://www.dozr.com.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for someone to join us as a Finance & Accounting Manager/Lead.

In this role you'll ensure that we can make business decisions off of timely, accurate and effective financial data. This means ensuring we are collecting the right data, we are booking that data accurately, reporting it effectively, and staying on top of our compliance obligations so that they do not slow us down. This is a full-time, remote-first role in Canada, the UK or the EU.





THINGS YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Accounting Ownership: you'll ensure that our team is able to close the month within 3 business days. This means booking revenue/expenses from various systems, applying inter-company transfer pricing, making accruals where needed, performing journal entries, etc. We close the books on 15 entities globally.

 - Financial Reporting: you'll prepare individual reporting for distinct legal entities, as well as a consolidated view for operating groups and the group as a whole. This will include Income Statements, Balance Sheets, Cashflow Statements and other management reporting.

 - Tax Coordination: we conduct business throughout Canada, the USA and the EU. You'll support with sales tax/VAT calculations, reporting and filing. You'll coordinate with our external tax partners to run year-end prep, corporate filings and tax optimization projects.

 - Automation & Scaleable Systems: you'll embrace technology, modern tooling and AI to automate and augment as much as you can. We don't want to replace people, but we do want to replace monotonous, manual, repetitive work.


ATTITUDES

We don't care what your current job title is, how long you've been working, or whether you have a CPA. We do care about you being tightly aligned with our operating principles:

 - You’re competitive and love to win. You’re competitive and are always looking for an edge. You have a high bar (some would say “unrealistic”) for yourself and those you work with.

 - You do whatever it takes. You’re willing to do what others won’t to achieve what others can’t. What we’re doing is hard. Really hard. Sacrifices are required in the pursuit of greatness, and we’re chasing greatness.

 - Founder-level ownership. If something breaks, you fix it. If there’s an opportunity, you chase it. You (and we) never say "that's not my job".

 - Curiosity with teeth. You don’t just ask why something isn’t working — you dig until you know, then you ship a fix.

 - You ask for forgiveness, not permission.

 - You’re a self-starter. You love being in the weeds and rolling up your sleeves to get things done. You never let roadblocks get in the way of getting things done; the laws of physics alone dictate what you can do.

 - You have excellent analytical skills. The term ‘quantitative rigour’ gets you excited. You love using data to help identify problems, discover opportunities, and substantiate your ideas.


WHO THIS ROLE IS RIGHT FOR

 - Someone who has a foundation in finance & accounting and is ready to take their next step and go both broader + deeper in their work

 - Someone who enjoys building automations and systems, and likes to work on unique/complex problems

 - Someone who wants to not just rebuild a finance function, but set it up to scal

source_jobs.title: Inside Sales Representative
source_jobs.description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)
We’re a team of ~50 people who’ve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. We’re a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist http://www.italist.com, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com and Verishop https://www.instagram.com/verishop/?hl=en, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight https://stylight.com and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai https://shopbonsai.ca, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress https://everpress.com and DOZR http://www.dozr.com.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for someone to join DOZR http://www.dozr.com as an Inside Sales Representative.

DOZR is an online marketplace that helps contractors and DIY'ers rent the construction equipment they need, where and when they need it. In this role you'll handle our inbound sales calls to help new and existing customers to find the right equipment and place their order.

This is a Remote role in Canada.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Receive dozens of inbound calls per day from new and existing customers across North America

 - Partner with customers to help them select the right equipment for their job, and then help them set up their account and place their rental order

 - Coordinate order scheduling, order quoting, payment processing, and invoice set-up

 - Follow up with customers we've quoted to help close the deal


ATTITUDES

We don't care what your current job title is, how long you've been working, or where you are. We do care about you being tightly aligned with our operating principles:

 - You’re competitive and love to win. You’re competitive and are always looking for an edge. You have a high bar (some would say “unrealistic”) for yourself and those you work with.

 - You do whatever it takes. You’re willing to do what others won’t to achieve what others can’t. What we’re doing is hard. Really hard. Sacrifices are required in the pursuit of greatness, and we’re chasing greatness.

 - You ask for forgiveness, not permission.

 - You’re a self-starter. You love being in the weeds and rolling up your sleeves to get things done. You never let roadblocks get in the way of getting things done; the laws of physics alone dictate what you can do.


WHO THIS ROLE IS RIGHT FOR

 - Someone who has worked in the construction/landscaping/equipment/industrial space, and is interested in growing as a sales person

 - Someone who enjoys working with people, being on calls most of the day, and solving problems collaboratively

 - Someone who's competitive and enjoys learning/growing and winning

 - Someone who is motivated by commissions and strong variable incentives


WHO THIS ROLE IS NOT RIGHT FOR

 - People who don't enjoy talking on the phone

 - People who get drained by dealing with people all day, everyday

 - People who are afraid to push for a sale and close deals


LOGISTICS/NEXT STEPS

 - Process: everyone will hear back within 3 days of applying. Our process involves calls with 2-3 members of our team.

 - Work Setup: we operate Remote-first in Canada.

 - Working Hours: there is some scheduling flexibility (depending on if you prefer an earlier or later workday), but for the most part your day will start between 8:00am-10:00am and finish between 5:00pm-7:00pm.

source_jobs.title: Summer Intern (Ecommerce Operations)
source_jobs.description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)
We’re a team of ~50 people who’ve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. We’re a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist http://www.italist.com, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com and Verishop https://www.instagram.com/verishop/?hl=en, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight https://stylight.com and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai https://shopbonsai.ca, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress https://everpress.com and DOZR http://www.dozr.com.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for someone to join us as a Summer Intern (Ecommerce Operations) on our Italist luxury fashion marketplace https://www.italist.com/.

Throughout the summer you will pilot a number of projects we have in our backlog, testing and experimenting to see what works (that we'll then invest in long-term) vs. what doesn't. You'll have the opportunity to leverage AI in your work, and to develop your skillset across Account Management, Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO), Pricing Strategy and Data Analysis/Business Intelligence.

This is a hands-on role - you will not be building decks and plans, you'll be taking project briefs and doing the actual work. This is full-time over the summer, and has the opportunity to convert to full-time or part-time after the internship. We are open to both current students, new grads, and folks making a career pivot or returning from a leave.

This is a full-time, remote-first role in Canada, the UK or the EU.





THINGS YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Piloting Projects: we have a backlog of projects we're looking to pilot across the business — you'll take project briefs, launch new initiatives quickly, iterate based on data, and then present your results so we can decide what to double-down on vs. what to pass on for now.

 - Reporting & Data Analysis: you'll dig deep into data across our business to draw insights and make data-backed recommendations on actions we can take. You'll learn to query data using SQL via AI interfaces, build reporting and dashboards, and set up alerting/monitoring with various integrations.

 - Building with AI: we are in the process of becoming more AI-first as an organization, and you'll be part of that transformation. You'll write code, build prototypes, manage API integrations, create automations and more through Claude Cowork, Cursor, etc.


ATTITUDES

We don't care what your current job title is, how long you've been working, or whether you have a pedigreed background. We do care about you being tightly aligned with our operating principles:

 - You’re competitive and love to win. You’re competitive and are always looking for an edge. You have a high bar (some would say “unrealistic”) for yourself and those you work with.

 - You do whatever it takes. You’re willing to do what others won’t to achieve what others can’t. What we’re doing is hard. Really hard. Sacrifices are required in the pursuit of greatness, and we’re chasing greatness.

 - Founder-level ownership. If something breaks, you fix it. If there’s an opportunity, you chase it. You (and we) never say "that's not my job".

 - Curiosity with teeth. You don’t just ask why something isn’t working — you dig until you know, then you ship a fix.

 - You ask for forgiveness, not permission.

 - You’re a self-starter. You love being in the weeds and rolling up your sleeves to get things done. You never let roadblocks get in the way of getting things done; the laws of physics alone dictate what you can do.


WHO THIS ROLE IS RIGHT FOR

 - Someone who has been building with AI on the side and is excited to apply that to real-world problems in a scaled business

 - Someone who has thought about running their own business, launching a startup, and being entrepreneurial

source_jobs.title: Finance Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)
We’re a team of ~50 people who’ve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. We’re a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist http://www.italist.com, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com and Verishop https://www.instagram.com/verishop/?hl=en, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight https://stylight.com and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai https://shopbonsai.ca, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress https://everpress.com and DOZR http://www.dozr.com.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for someone to join DOZR http://www.dozr.com/ as a Finance Coordinator.

DOZR is a two-sided marketplace that helps contractors and DIYers rent the construction equipment they need, where and when they need it. Every rental on DOZR becomes an invoice, a payment, a supplier payout — and occasionally a damage claim. In this role you'll support the day-to-day financial operations that keep the marketplace running: billing customers, collecting payments, paying suppliers and keeping our books clean. Your job is to make sure money moves accurately and on time, in both directions.

This is a full-time, remote-first role based in Canada.





THINGS YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Invoicing & Billing: you'll own customer billing end-to-end — generating accurate invoices for rentals (including extensions, overages, fuel and damage charges), issuing credits and adjustments when needed, and resolving billing disputes quickly and fairly.

 - AR & Collections: you'll run our receivables motion — monitoring aging, chasing down payment by phone and email, coordinating with our sales team on tricky accounts, and keeping DSO moving in the right direction. Construction is an industry of slow payers; your job is to make DOZR the invoice that gets paid first.

 - AP & Bank Payments: you'll prepare bank payment runs and supplier payouts, help process vendor bills, and make sure the businesses that rent out their equipment through us get paid accurately and on time. Suppliers trust us with million-dollar fleets — paying them reliably is core to that trust.

 - Sales Commissions: you'll own the monthly commissions process for our sales team — calculating payouts, validating the underlying deal data, resolving questions, and getting everything to payroll on time.

 - Bookkeeping Support: you'll support month-end close — coding transactions, reconciling accounts and payment processors, and helping with journal entries so we can close fast and trust the numbers.

 - Insurance Claims: you'll support claims when equipment is damaged or lost — gathering documentation, filing and tracking claims with insurers, and following each one through to recovery.

 - Automation & Better Systems: you'll embrace technology, modern tooling and AI to automate the repetitive parts of all of the above. We don't want to replace people, but we do want to replace monotonous, manual, repetitive work.


ATTITUDES

We don't care what your current job title is, how long you've been working, or whether you have a CPA. We do care about you being tightly aligned with our operating principles:

 - You're competitive and love to win. You're competitive and are always looking for an edge. You have a high bar (some would say "unrealistic") for yourself and those you work with.

 - You do whatever it takes. You're willing to do what others won't to achieve what others can't. What we're doing is hard. Really hard. Sacrifices are required in the pursuit of greatness, and we're chasing greatness.

 - Founder-level ownership. If something breaks, you fix it. If there's an opportunity, you chase it. You (and we) never say "that's not my job".

 - Curiosity with teeth. You don't just ask why something isn't working — you dig until you know,

source_jobs.title: Lifecycle Marketing Lead (Luxury Fashion)
source_jobs.description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)
We’re a team of ~50 people who’ve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. We’re a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist http://www.italist.com, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com and Verishop https://www.instagram.com/verishop/?hl=en, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight https://stylight.com and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai https://shopbonsai.ca, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress https://everpress.com and DOZR http://www.dozr.com.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for someone to join italist http://www.italist.com/ as a Lifecycle/Ecommerce Marketing Lead.

italist is a leading luxury fashion marketplace, connecting shoppers around the world with the best luxury boutiques across Italy and Europe. Acquisition gets customers to italist once — your job is everything after that. You'll own customer loyalty, retention and re-engagement, and our owned channels: email and social. If a customer buys once and never comes back, that's on you; if they become a loyal repeat shopper who opens every email, that's on you too!

This is a full-time, remote-first role in Canada, the US or the UK.





THINGS YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Email & Lifecycle Marketing: you'll own our email program in Klaviyo end-to-end — the campaign calendar, trigger-based flows (welcome, browse and cart abandonment, post-purchase, winback), segmentation, testing and deliverability. Roughly 20% of italist's revenue comes from email today; you'll own that number and push it higher — and prove it with holdouts and incrementality, not just last-click attribution.

 - Retention & Re-engagement: you'll own repeat purchase rate — building the journeys that turn a first order into a second, and re-activating customers who've gone quiet. In luxury, the second purchase is where the economics really start to work.

 - Loyalty Program Relaunch: you'll relaunch our loyalty program end-to-end — positioning, mechanics, build (across Shopify and Klaviyo), launch and measurement — and make it something a luxury customer actually cares about, not a points scheme they ignore.

 - Personalization: you'll personalize our marketing campaigns and on-site experience — using browsing behaviour, purchase history and brand affinities so every email and landing page feels curated by a personal shopper, not batched by a system.

 - Owned Social: you'll run our organic social presence as a true owned channel — content that builds the brand, feeds the funnel, and gives customers a reason to follow us between purchases.

 - AI Leverage: you'll use AI tooling and agentic workflows to get more out of everything above — drafting, building, analyzing and personalizing at a scale one marketer couldn't touch a few years ago. 1-2 people with the right systems should ship what used to take an entire team.


ATTITUDES

We don't care what your current job title is, how long you've been working, or whether you've worked in luxury before. We do care about you being tightly aligned with our operating principles:

 - You're competitive and love to win. You're competitive and are always looking for an edge. You have a high bar (some would say "unrealistic") for yourself and those you work with.

 - You do whatever it takes. You're willing to do what others won't to achieve what others can't. What we're doing is hard. Really hard. Sacrifices are required in the pursuit of greatness, and we're chasing greatness.

 - Founder-level ownership. If something breaks, you fix it. If there's an opportunity, you chase it. You (and we) never say "that's not my job".

 - Curiosity with teeth. You don't just ask why something isn't working — you dig until you know, then you ship a fix.

 - You ask for forgiveness, not permission.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)
We’re a team of ~50 people who’ve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. We’re a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist http://www.italist.com, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com and Verishop https://www.instagram.com/verishop/?hl=en, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight https://stylight.com and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai https://shopbonsai.ca, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress https://everpress.com and DOZR http://www.dozr.com.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for someone to join us as a Senior Software Engineer, working across our marketplace businesses — italist http://www.italist.com/, HEWI http://www.hardlyeverwornit.com/ and DOZR http://www.dozr.com/.

This is not a role where a product manager hands you a ticket and holds your hand to the finish line — there is no product manager. You'll talk directly to the people who need something built, figure out what actually needs to exist, then build it, test it, deploy it, and validate that it's working in production and moving the number it was supposed to move. You own the outcome, not the pull request.

This is a full-time, remote-first role — you can be located anywhere in the world.





THINGS YOU'LL WORK ON

 - End-to-End Feature Ownership: you'll take features from a conversation to production — scoping directly with stakeholders (GMs, marketers, operators), building, testing, shipping, and then checking your own work: is it live, is it working, did it have the intended effect? "Merged" isn't done. Done is validated and in customers' hands.

 - Full-Stack Development: you'll build in JavaScript across the stack — React on the frontend, Node on the backend. The surfaces vary: customer-facing storefront features, internal admin tools, third-party integrations, data pipelines and automations that keep marketplaces running.

 - Working Across Businesses: one week you might be shipping a personalization feature for a luxury fashion marketplace, the next building supplier tooling for heavy equipment rentals. Three businesses, three domains, one engineer who can context-switch between them and carry patterns from one to the others.

 - AI-Native Engineering: you'll work with Claude Code or another AI copilot as your daily driver — not autocomplete, but genuine agentic workflows: parallelizing work, generating and reviewing code, writing tests, automating the repetitive parts. We expect AI to make you dramatically faster and more rigorous, and we invest in the tooling to make that true.


ATTITUDES

We don't care what your current job title is, how long you've been working, or whether you have a CS degree. We do care about you being tightly aligned with our operating principles:

 - You're competitive and love to win. You're competitive and are always looking for an edge. You have a high bar (some would say "unrealistic") for yourself and those you work with.

 - You do whatever it takes. You're willing to do what others won't to achieve what others can't. What we're doing is hard. Really hard. Sacrifices are required in the pursuit of greatness, and we're chasing greatness.

 - Founder-level ownership. If something breaks, you fix it. If there's an opportunity, you chase it. You (and we) never say "that's not my job".

 - Curiosity with teeth. You don't just ask why something isn't working — you dig until you know, then you ship a fix.

 - You ask for forgiveness, not permission.

 - You're a self-starter. You love being in the weeds and rolling up your sleeves to get things done. You never let roadblocks get in the way of getting things done; the laws of physics alone dictate what you can do.

 - You have excellent analytical skills. The term 'quantitative rigour' gets
